title: astragalus muelleri subsp. muelleri
intro: Astragalus muelleri subsp. muelleri, also known as the Alpine milk-vetch, is a flowering plant in the legume family native to the highlands of New South Wales in Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties, with medicinal compounds such as flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids contributing to its therapeutic potential. The health benefits of Astragalus muelleri subsp. muelleri are believed to include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and cardiovascular diseases. Astragalus muelleri subsp. muelleri can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.
